title: Varaita Valley

infobox.value: (in [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") *Valle Varaita*) is a valley in south-west of [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont"), in the [Province of Cuneo](/wiki/Province_of_Cuneo "Province of Cuneo") - [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy").

**Varaita Valley** (in [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") *Valle Varaita*) is a valley in south-west of [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont"), in the [Province of Cuneo](/wiki/Province_of_Cuneo "Province of Cuneo") - [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy").

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Varaita_Valley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

It takes its name from the [Varaita](/wiki/Varaita "Varaita"), a right-hand [tributary](/wiki/Tributary "Tributary") of the [Po](/wiki/Po_(river) "Po (river)") which flows through the valley.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Varaita_Valley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Close the French border the valley is divided in two sub-valleys named *Valle di Bellino* (south) and *Valle di Chianale* (north), which meet near [Casteldelfino](/wiki/Casteldelfino "Casteldelfino").

The [municipalities](/wiki/Comune "Comune") of Varaita Valley are [Bellino](/wiki/Bellino "Bellino"), [Brossasco](/wiki/Brossasco "Brossasco"), [Casteldelfino](/wiki/Casteldelfino "Casteldelfino"), [Costigliole Saluzzo](/wiki/Costigliole_Saluzzo "Costigliole Saluzzo"), [Frassino](/wiki/Frassino "Frassino"), [Isasca](/wiki/Isasca "Isasca"), [Melle](/wiki/Melle,_Piedmont "Melle, Piedmont"), [Piasco](/wiki/Piasco "Piasco"), [Pontechianale](/wiki/Pontechianale "Pontechianale"), [Rossana](/wiki/Rossana,_Piedmont "Rossana, Piedmont"), [Sampeyre](/wiki/Sampeyre "Sampeyre"), [Valmala](/wiki/Valmala,_Italy "Valmala, Italy"), [Venasca](/wiki/Venasca "Venasca") and [Verzuolo](/wiki/Verzuolo "Verzuolo").

## Notable summits

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Varaita_Valley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notable summits")]

The most important mountains of the valley are the [Monviso](/wiki/Monviso "Monviso") (3.841 m) and its subpeaks.

Other relevant summits of the valley, all belonging to the [Cottian Alps](/wiki/Cottian_Alps "Cottian Alps"), are:

* Valle di Chianale:
  + Monte Losetta - 3054 m
  + Monte Ruine - 3143 m
  + Monte Aiguillette - 3298 m
  + Pic d'Asti - 3219 m
  + Rocca Bianca - 3064 m
  + Monte Pelvo - 3024 m
* Valle di Bellino:
  + [Mongioia](/wiki/Mongioia "Mongioia") - 3340 m
  + Testa di Malacosta - 3212 m
  + [Monte Ferra](/wiki/Monte_Ferra "Monte Ferra") - 3094 m
  + Testa di Malacosta - 3212 m
  + Pelvo di Ciabrera - 3152 m
  + [Pelvo d'Elva](/wiki/Pelvo_d%27Elva "Pelvo d'Elva") - 3046 m
* Mid and lowest part of the valley:
  + Punta Rasciassa - 2664 m
  + [Monte Nebin](/wiki/Monte_Nebin "Monte Nebin") - 2510 m
  + Testa di Garitta Nuova - 2385 m
  + [Monte Birrone](/wiki/Monte_Birrone "Monte Birrone") - 2131 m
  + Monte Ricordone - 1764 m
  + Monte san Bernardo - 1625 m

## Access

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Varaita_Valley&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Access")]

The valley can be reached by car/bus from [Po Plain](/wiki/Po_Plain "Po Plain") following the *strada provinciale*[[1]](#cite_note-1) *della Valle Varaita*; it also can be accessed by the French region of [Queyras](/wiki/Queyras "Queyras") by the [Colle dell'Agnello](/wiki/Colle_dell%27Agnello "Colle dell'Agnello"), the third highest Alpine pass crossed by an asphalted road.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
